transcript.clean_text: well after the Mayhem that we had on stage eight with 30 Riders up the road finally neutralized would a breakaway make it up the road going all in as you will ahead of the rest day well the parkour certainly suggested it could happen when you saw the wind as well Crosswinds were going to be an issue during the day it meant this was going to be a lively start not least because the wind was blowing in from the East which meant a Tailwind to get everybody going there on the rollers before the start this was at least going to feature a breakaway of what composition nobody quite knew SE cus resplendant in the leaders jersey backed up by a highly efficient yumbo visma team that looked like they're on the way to winning just about everything at the moment well the first categorized climb came early in the first third of the r for a full distance of 185 km but really it was the sequence of walls that made up the final climb in a many steps that really drew the eye so the breakaways were each neutralized again and again and almost by accident yumbo visma found themselves accidentally up front with six Riders towards the four along with Remco aapol and katano from sudal quickstep well the pel must have thought to themselves isn't it supposed to be us up the road and and you guys chasing in well order was achieved about 10 or 15 km later a very strong Breakaway went up the road but the big factor of the day was indeed the wind huge splits opened up as Crosswinds had an impact and the big names were caught out including Lenny Martinez former leader with group armor Francis deer they were battling to close the gap Bor hrow joining forces with yumbo visma not chasing down their own man Lenny kemna who was up front but trying to get a margin for Alexander vlasov in the overall standings well honors even the big groups regathered themselves but heavy rain at the finish and mud on the course meant that the organizers declared the finish line for the GC men to be 2 km from the finale so less stress perhaps and then less importance in catching the Breakaway with those bonus seconds disappearing up the road it was down to these men to decide who would get there first stage win up for grabs then for those at the head of the road and 5 minutes now further back it was all about the GC running for kilometer two to go then kemna hit the climb with eight remaining so the full distance of this to be contested by the Breakaway Chris Hamilton was the first to respond for DSM feric doing a great job but ultimately he was to run out of Resort hes coming through mattio saero it was still getting K Gabby shabier from Little Tre was also very much involved the GC battle well it was all about honor really and Remco Evol was one of those that decided he'd like to test rogl meanwhile up front 4.8 km to go Lenny kemner left saero nearest man to him and then just had to deal with ramps of around 20 23% all the way home a big ask well with a Finish Line looming for those further down the mountain it was uzo that went for it looking for bonus seconds Alexander vlasov set off as well thinking oh you know what it's going to be tightly packed around the top 10 why should I not be part of that just behind was that group with Evan Appo And Rog litch who were about to get into fist cups there was the GC Finish Line as declared but still a stage win up for grabs this was rogich responding to an attack by Evan aul there would be a couple of seconds between them by the end but nothing too bad ahead of a rest day and of course a time tral so the GC battle had been declared at the foot of the final ramp but out front there was a man who was looking to write pages in his own history book except C saying thank you very much for to teammates around him kemna was pushing on already a stage winner in the jir Italia and the Tour of France he puts this in his back pocket as well the full set belongs to the young German and how delighted was he coming home second mattio soero but there was some big gaps even amongst the Breakaway the heat was out of the GC battle and they rolled in with smiles upon their faces none broader though than that on the Visage of Lenny kemner he' done it full set of stage wins one in each of the ground tours and possibly more to come than this man who regards himself as a breakaway King well all the others came home through the clouds through the mud and indeed got the Applause that they duly deserve seus is our leader holds the same margin as the start of the day going into the rest day and of course that run around viid and the time trial how will sepkus stand up well he says he's going to give it his best go VI doids big transfer to get there and we're just hoping the rain stays away it's quite a technical circuit in places plenty of 90° turns along the way and 25.8 km to be dealt with first though the very important duty of having a break before the hostilities begin in This Magnificent tour
